concerns.text: The plan assumes the OpenNeuro ds000277 dataset contains a 'validated working-memory task' baseline score. ds000277 (HCP S1200) primarily contains resting-state fMRI and structural data; the specific behavioral phenotypic file containing a 'baseline working memory score' (distinct from the N-back task used in other HCP releases) is not guaranteed to exist in the standard phenotypic TSV. The plan treats the existence of this specific variable as a certainty ('Assumptions' section) rather than verifying its presence in the dataset's actual schema. If the column is missing, the regression (FR-005) will fail, invalidating the research question.

The plan targets a GitHub Actions free-tier runner (2 cores, 7GB RAM) to run fMRIPrep 23.1.3 on N=85 participants within 24 hours. fMRIPrep is a heavy Docker container requiring significant RAM (often >8GB for full preprocessing) and CPU time. Running it sequentially on a 2-core/7GB runner for 85 subjects is computationally infeasible; a single subject often exceeds the 24h total budget or the 7GB RAM limit, causing OOM kills. The plan's 'sequential processing' mitigation does not resolve the fundamental resource mismatch between fMRIPrep's requirements and the CI runner's constraints.
